---
title: "Find spatial niches"
description: "NicheCompass niche identification."
categories: [openpipeline_spatial]
order: 70
---

[`openpipeline_spatial`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline_spatial}<span class="swatch openpipeline_spatial"></span>

A **niche** is a recurring spatial neighborhood — a local mix of cell types that
appears across a tissue. The **`openpipeline_spatial`** package uses
[NicheCompass](https://nichecompass.readthedocs.io/) to learn these
neighborhoods from a spatial `.h5mu`.

## Prerequisite

Start from an ingested, processed spatial object (see
[Ingest Xenium / Visium](ingest-spatial.qmd)) that carries spot/cell coordinates
in `.obsm["spatial"]`.

## Build the gene-program mask

NicheCompass models niches on top of curated gene programs (sets of genes that
act together). The `nichecompass/gene_program_mask` component prepares that mask,
which the NicheCompass workflow then uses to embed cells into a niche-aware
latent space and cluster them into niches.

:::{.callout-note}
NicheCompass niche identification is specific to `openpipeline_spatial`. See the
[reference](../reference/index.qmd) (filter by `openpipeline_spatial`) for the
NicheCompass components and their arguments, and open the package's Viash Hub
page for run instructions.
:::

**Next:** [spatial reference →](../reference/index.qmd)
